title: VOC Port and India's NTPC Will Work Together to Develop 1 MW of Green Hydrogen
content: V. O. Chidambaranar Port (VOC Port) in Thoothukudi is set to sign an MoU with NTPC to develop a 1 MW green hydrogen facility, marking a major step toward sustainable maritime infrastructure. This is part of a broader strategy that includes a 1 MW solar project under implementation, a 6 MW wind project in tendering, a planned 2 MW battery storage system, and upcoming green methanol bunkering facilities. The port is actively working with global partners like the EU and Ports of Singapore and Rotterdam to create green shipping corridors. Itâ€™s also exploring innovative renewable technologies like Wind Trees, Solar Trees, and offshore wind energy. Additionally, a Shore-to-Ship Power Supply project is underway to cut emissions from berthed vessels. Together, these initiatives position VOC Port as a leading model for eco-conscious, future-ready port development in India.
author: Analysts Account
